paper_title: Ice Load Scenarios and Data Analysis for GIA Modeling

methods.description: Development of ice load scenarios to drive the Glacial Isostatic Adjustment (GIA) model, investigating the solid Earth response to different historical ice loading events.

steps.description: The KIS stagnation scenarios are used in isolation to investigate the solid Earth response to this most recent loading event.
steps.searchable_summary: Use KIS stagnation scenarios to model solid Earth response

steps.description: The regional scenario combines the Antarctic-wide W12 ice load model with a detailed history of Siple Coast ice loading during the last 2000 years.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine W12 model with Siple Coast history for GIA modeling

steps.description: An additional ice loading history is constructed with accelerated grounding line retreat in the Ross Sea component of the W12 model.
steps.searchable_summary: Construct accelerated Ross Sea retreat model for GIA
inputs: Ross Sea component of W12 model

methods.description: Analysis of Surface Mass Balance (SMB) data to determine ice thickening distribution and magnitude along KIS.

steps.description: SMB data are available from the regional atmospheric climate model RACMO2.1/ANT.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch SMB data from RACMO2.1/ANT model

steps.description: Average SMB rates for the epoch were used when calculating the recent ice loading history of the Siple Coast.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate average SMB rates for Siple Coast ice loading history

methods.method_name: Ice Surface Elevation Change Data Analysis
methods.description: Use of surface elevation change data to constrain reconstructions of recent ice thickness change across the Siple Coast.

steps.description: Data are available from ICESat laser altimetry and CryoSat-2 radar altimetry measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch surface elevation change data from ICESat and CryoSat-2

steps.description: ICESat data were used to constrain our modeling.
steps.searchable_summary: Use ICESat data to constrain ice thickness change modeling

methods.description: Comparison of GIA modeling results with GPS-observed present-day uplift rates.

steps.description: The results of the GIA modeling were compared with GPS-observed present-day uplift rates at five sites in the region.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare GIA model results with GPS uplift rates
